_about: conductor-code verification suite: WHAT to run, WHEN, HOW it feeds the hill-climb score, and the test ENVIRONMENT. Copied to .conductor-code/checks.json by `adopt`; project-specific. This is how the harness verifies real runtime behavior (concurrency, distributed correctness), not just a green unit build.

environment._about: How the `provision_env` worker stands up the system under test for checks marked needsEnv. `up` before, `down` after; ready when readyCheck passes.
environment._alt: k8s/kind, testcontainers, or a remote cluster are equally valid — only up/down/readyCheck change.

checks._about: Each check: kind, cmd, `when` it runs (per-round | per-wave | final), `blocking`, `weight` within the verification signal, `needsEnv`, and non-determinism handling (`nonDeterministic` + `reruns`). Altitudes keep expensive distributed checks out of the per-round hill-climb.

invariants._about: System-wide properties the change MUST preserve; each maps to the check(s) that prove it. Verified at the walking skeleton (DESIGN §4.2), per-wave, and the final review. A failing invariant bounces back to DESIGN, not just implement.

no-loss-on-single-failure.desc: no acknowledged write lost if one node dies

flake._about: Non-deterministic checks are re-run `reruns` times; a result is a pass only if pass-rate >= passRate. The score uses the pass-rate, and deltas within noiseBand do NOT count as score improvement — so flaky results can't fool keep-best or plateau detection. Keep reviewLoop.plateau.minImprovement >= noiseBand.
